items.title: Agent spec
items.description: The manifest format for agents, tools, and policies.
items.content: Agent spec The manifest format for agents, tools, and policies. The Phrony Agent Spec is an open standard for production agents. Declare an agent in a versioned manifest; a compliant runtime loads it on every run, enforces what it allows, and emits a structured trace.

items.title: SDKs
items.description: Client libraries for the Phrony runtime over gRPC.
items.content: SDKs Client libraries for the Phrony runtime over gRPC. Phrony SDKs wrap the runtime gRPC API so you can run agents and bundles, stream interactive sessions, and register tool workers from your application code. The contract matches the{' '} runtime {' '} service — unary RPCs for lifecycle and catalog operations, plus bidirectional streams for{' '} Work (tool workers) and RunSessionInteractive (streaming sessions). Available SDKs

items.title: Available SDKs
items.content: Available SDKs SDKs Client libraries for the Phrony runtime over gRPC. Phrony SDKs wrap the runtime gRPC API so you can run agents and bundles, stream interactive sessions, and register tool workers from your application code. The contract matches the{' '} runtime {' '} service — unary RPCs for lifecycle and catalog operations, plus bidirectional streams for{' '} Work (tool workers) and RunSessionInteractive (streaming sessions).

items.title: TypeScript SDK
items.description: The @phrony/sdk client for Node.js — install, run agents and bundles, stream sessions, and register workers.
items.content: TypeScript SDK The @phrony/sdk client for Node.js — install, run agents and bundles, stream sessions, and register workers. @phrony/sdk is the TypeScript client for the Phrony runtime. It targets Node.js 18+, uses{' '} @grpc/grpc-js , and maps proto bytes JSON fields through helpers so you work with ordinary JavaScript objects. Install from your package registry, then{' '} connect to a runtime . For a guided tool-binding walkthrough, see{' '} Add a tool binding . Every SDK entrypoint dials the runtime over gRPC. Set PHRONY_RUNTIME_ADDR or pass an explicit address. After{' '} installing {' '} the package, use one of the patterns below. Phrony is the high-level entry point for agent and bundle sessions. Connect first — see{' '} Connect to the runtime . Multi-agent systems published as a{' '} Bundle {' '} run through the bundle root member. PhronyBundle mirrors PhronyAgent — same{' '} run() options and AgentSessionError on failure. For streaming event types, see{' '} Interactive sessions . On failure with wait: true , run() throws AgentSessionError — see{' '} Utilities . InteractiveSession wraps RunSessionInteractive . Open it from{' '} RuntimeClient.runSessionInteractive() or{' '} PhronyAgent.runInteractive() {' '} or PhronyBundle.runInteractive() . The first client message must be start or{' '} attach . Server events are a discriminated union on type : session_started , completed , failed , cancelled ,{' '} stream_end text_delta , awaiting_input tool_call , tool_result , approval_required Workers implement tools declared on agents over the Work stream. See{' '} Tool workers {' '} in the runtime docs. Low-level helper from @phrony/sdk/worker for custom registration or reconnect logic. RuntimeClient exposes every runtime unary RPC. Connect via{' '} Connect to the runtime . For agent runs, prefer{' '} Phrony . Bundle lifecycle RPCs mirror the CLI{' '} bundles {' '} commands. Publish requires the committed bundle.lock.json bytes alongside the bundle manifest. Import proto message types from @phrony/sdk when building requests for{' '} RuntimeClient unary methods. AgentRef , BundleRef , Approval , ApprovalDecision ,{' '} PublishRequest , PublishBundleRequest , DeployRequest ,{' '} DeployBundleRequest , BundleSummary , BundleVersionSummary ,{' '} RunSessionRequest , WorkClientMsg , RunSessionInteractiveClientMsg Install Package Import paths Connect to the runtime RuntimeClient Phrony Address resolution TLS Health check Cleanup Run agents & bundles Phrony PhronyAgent PhronyBundle Interactive sessions Methods InteractiveEvent types Lifecycle Streaming Tools Tool workers Worker WorkStream Runtime client Version and sessions Publish and deploy — agents Publish and deploy — bundles Catalog Approvals Streams Utilities Agent references Bundle references JSON bytes Errors Constants and helpers Generated types Commonly used types

items.title: Connect to the runtime
items.content: Connect to the runtime TypeScript SDK The @phrony/sdk client for Node.js — install, run agents and bundles, stream sessions, and register workers. @phrony/sdk is the TypeScript client for the Phrony runtime. It targets Node.js 18+, uses{' '} @grpc/grpc-js , and maps proto bytes JSON fields through helpers so you work with ordinary JavaScript objects. Install from your package registry, then{' '} connect to a runtime . For a guided tool-binding walkthrough, see{' '} Add a tool binding . Every SDK entrypoint dials the runtime over gRPC. Set PHRONY_RUNTIME_ADDR or pass an explicit address. After{' '} installing {' '} the package, use one of the patterns below. Phrony is the high-level entry point for agent and bundle sessions. Connect first — see{' '} Connect to the runtime . Multi-agent systems published as a{' '} Bundle {' '} run through the bundle root member. PhronyBundle mirrors PhronyAgent — same{' '} run() options and AgentSessionError on failure. For streaming event types, see{' '} Interactive sessions . On failure with wait: true , run() throws AgentSessionError — see{' '} Utilities . InteractiveSession wraps RunSessionInteractive . Open it from{' '} RuntimeClient.runSessionInteractive() or{' '} PhronyAgent.runInteractive() {' '} or PhronyBundle.runInteractive() . The first client message must be start or{' '} attach . Server events are a discriminated union on type : session_started , completed , failed , cancelled ,{' '} stream_end text_delta , awaiting_input tool_call , tool_result , approval_required Workers implement tools declared on agents over the Work stream. See{' '} Tool workers {' '} in the runtime docs. Low-level helper from @phrony/sdk/worker for custom registration or reconnect logic. RuntimeClient exposes every runtime unary RPC. Connect via{' '} Connect to the runtime . For agent runs, prefer{' '} Phrony . Bundle lifecycle RPCs mirror the CLI{' '} bundles {' '} commands. Publish requires the committed bundle.lock.json bytes alongside the bundle manifest. Import proto message types from @phrony/sdk when building requests for{' '} RuntimeClient unary methods. Set PHRONY_RUNTIME_ADDR or pass an explicit address. After{' '} installing {' '} the package, use one of the patterns below. Phrony is the high-level entry point for agent and bundle sessions. It targets Node.js 18+, uses{' '} @grpc/grpc-js , and maps proto bytes JSON fields through helpers so you work with ordinary JavaScript objects. Install from your package registry, then{' '} connect to a runtime . For a guided tool-binding walkthrough, see{' '} Add a tool binding . Every SDK entrypoint dials the runtime over gRPC. Set PHRONY_RUNTIME_ADDR or pass an explicit address. After{' '} installing {' '} the package, use one of the patterns below. Phrony is the high-level entry point for agent and bundle sessions. Connect first — see{' '} Connect to the runtime . Multi-agent systems published as a{' '} Bundle {' '} run through the bundle root member. PhronyBundle mirrors PhronyAgent — same{' '} run() options and AgentSessionError on failure. For streaming event types, see{' '} Interactive sessions . On failure with wait: true , run() throws AgentSessionError — see{' '} Utilities . InteractiveSession wraps RunSessionInteractive . Open it from{' '} RuntimeClient.runSessionInteractive() or{' '} PhronyAgent.runInteractive() {' '} or PhronyBundle.runInteractive() . The first client message must be start or{' '} attach . Server events are a discriminated union on type : session_started , completed , failed , cancelled ,{' '} stream_end text_delta , awaiting_input tool_call , tool_result , approval_required Workers implement tools declared on agents over the Work stream. See{' '} Tool workers {' '} in the runtime docs. Low-level helper from @phrony/sdk/worker for custom registration or reconnect logic. RuntimeClient exposes every runtime unary RPC. Connect via{' '} Connect to the runtime . For agent runs, prefer{' '} Phrony . Bundle lifecycle RPCs mirror the CLI{' '} bundles {' '} commands. Publish requires the committed bundle.lock.json bytes alongside the bundle manifest. Import proto message types from @phrony/sdk when building requests for{' '} RuntimeClient unary methods. AgentRef , BundleRef , Approval , ApprovalDecision ,{' '} PublishRequest , PublishBundleRequest , DeployRequest ,{' '} DeployBundleRequest , BundleSummary , BundleVersionSummary ,{' '} RunSessionRequest , WorkClientMsg , RunSessionInteractiveClientMsg

items.title: Python SDK
items.description: The phrony client for Python — install, run agents and bundles, stream sessions, and register workers.
items.content: Python SDK The phrony client for Python — install, run agents and bundles, stream sessions, and register workers. phrony is the Python client for the Phrony runtime. It targets Python 3.10+, uses{' '} grpc.aio for async transport, and maps proto bytes JSON fields through helpers so you work with ordinary Python objects — strings and dicts for agent refs, keyword arguments on common RPCs, and flattened interactive events. All public APIs are async — run them with{' '} asyncio . Install from PyPI, then{' '} connect to a runtime . For a guided tool-binding walkthrough, see{' '} Add a tool binding . Every SDK entrypoint dials the runtime over gRPC. Set PHRONY_RUNTIME_ADDR or pass an explicit address. After{' '} installing {' '} the package, use one of the patterns below. All connection methods are async. Phrony is the high-level entry point for agent and bundle sessions. Connect first — see{' '} Connect to the runtime . Multi-agent systems published as a{' '} Bundle {' '} run through the bundle root member. PhronyBundle mirrors PhronyAgent — same{' '} run() options and AgentSessionError on failure. For streaming event types, see{' '} Interactive sessions . On failure with wait=True , run() raises AgentSessionError — see{' '} Utilities . InteractiveSession wraps RunSessionInteractive . Open it from{' '} RuntimeClient.run_session_interactive() or{' '} PhronyAgent.run_interactive() {' '} or PhronyBundle.run_interactive() . The first client message must be start or{' '} attach . Server events are dicts with a type key. Lifecycle and tool events expose flat fields — for example session_started includes session_id ,{' '} agent_version_id , and history ; approval_required includes{' '} approval_id , tool , and parsed args . session_started , completed , failed , cancelled ,{' '} stream_end text_delta , awaiting_input tool_call , tool_result , approval_required Workers implement tools declared on agents over the Work stream. See{' '} Tool workers {' '} in the runtime docs. Low-level helper from phrony.worker for custom registration or reconnect logic. RuntimeClient exposes every runtime unary RPC. Connect via{' '} Connect to the runtime . For agent runs, prefer{' '} Phrony . Bundle lifecycle RPCs mirror the CLI{' '} bundles {' '} commands. Publish requires the committed bundle.lock.json bytes alongside the bundle manifest. Import typed shapes from phrony for refs, interactive events, and common responses. Unary methods with keyword-arg convenience include run_session ,{' '} cancel_session , complete_session , inspect_session ,{' '} get_approval , decide_approval , and list_approvals . For publish, deploy, and catalog RPCs, pass protobuf request messages from{' '} phrony.gen.phrony.runtime.v1 — nested agent_ref and{' '} bundle_ref fields accept dicts from parse_agent_ref /{' '} parse_bundle_ref . AgentRef , BundleRef , InteractiveEvent ,{' '} InteractiveSessionStartOptions , ApprovalDecision ,{' '} GetVersionResponse , RunSessionResponse Install Package Import paths Connect to the runtime RuntimeClient Phrony Address resolution TLS Health check Cleanup Run agents & bundles Phrony PhronyAgent PhronyBundle Interactive sessions Methods InteractiveEvent types Lifecycle Streaming Tools Tool workers Worker WorkStream Runtime client Version and sessions Publish and deploy — agents Publish and deploy — bundles Catalog Approvals Streams Utilities Agent references Bundle references JSON bytes Errors Constants and helpers Generated types Commonly used types

It targets Python 3.10+, uses{' '} grpc.aio for async transport, and maps proto bytes JSON fields through helpers so you work with ordinary Python objects — strings and dicts for agent refs, keyword arguments on common RPCs, and flattened interactive events. All public APIs are async — run them with{' '} asyncio . Install from PyPI, then{' '} connect to a runtime . For a guided tool-binding walkthrough, see{' '} Add a tool binding . Every SDK entrypoint dials the runtime over gRPC. Set PHRONY_RUNTIME_ADDR or pass an explicit address. After{' '} installing {' '} the package, use one of the patterns below. All connection methods are async. Phrony is the high-level entry point for agent and bundle sessions. Connect first — see{' '} Connect to the runtime . Multi-agent systems published as a{' '} Bundle {' '} run through the bundle root member. PhronyBundle mirrors PhronyAgent — same{' '} run() options and AgentSessionError on failure. For streaming event types, see{' '} Interactive sessions . On failure with wait=True , run() raises AgentSessionError — see{' '} Utilities . InteractiveSession wraps RunSessionInteractive . Open it from{' '} RuntimeClient.run_session_interactive() or{' '} PhronyAgent.run_interactive() {' '} or PhronyBundle.run_interactive() . The first client message must be start or{' '} attach . Server events are dicts with a type key. Lifecycle and tool events expose flat fields — for example session_started includes session_id ,{' '} agent_version_id , and history ; approval_required includes{' '} approval_id , tool , and parsed args . session_started , completed , failed , cancelled ,{' '} stream_end text_delta , awaiting_input tool_call , tool_result , approval_required Workers implement tools declared on agents over the Work stream. See{' '} Tool workers {' '} in the runtime docs. Low-level helper from phrony.worker for custom registration or reconnect logic. RuntimeClient exposes every runtime unary RPC. Connect via{' '} Connect to the runtime . For agent runs, prefer{' '} Phrony . Bundle lifecycle RPCs mirror the CLI{' '} bundles {' '} commands. Publish requires the committed bundle.lock.json bytes alongside the bundle manifest. Import typed shapes from phrony for refs, interactive events, and common responses. Unary methods with keyword-arg convenience include run_session ,{' '} cancel_session , complete_session , inspect_session ,{' '} get_approval , decide_approval , and list_approvals . For publish, deploy, and catalog RPCs, pass protobuf request messages from{' '} phrony.gen.phrony.runtime.v1 — nested agent_ref and{' '} bundle_ref fields accept dicts from parse_agent_ref /{' '} parse_bundle_ref . AgentRef , BundleRef , InteractiveEvent ,{' '} InteractiveSessionStartOptions , ApprovalDecision ,{' '} GetVersionResponse , RunSessionResponse
